summary.name: Monthly Momentum Stocks TLT Risk-Off Filter
summary.author: Jing Wu
summary.description: The strategy is a monthly value plus momentum equity selection model with an explicit regime filter that shifts risk into long duration Treasuries during equity drawdown conditions. Each month it forms a U.S. large cap universe by first taking the most liquid stocks above 5 dollars, then restricting to firms with positive EV to EBITDA and market capitalization above roughly 2 billion dollars, and finally focusing on the 100 stocks with the highest EV to EBITDA (i.e., the most expensive on that m
summary.description_full:
The strategy is a monthly value plus momentum equity selection model with an explicit regime filter that shifts risk into long duration Treasuries during equity drawdown conditions. Each month it forms a U.S. large cap universe by first taking the most liquid stocks above 5 dollars, then restricting to firms with positive EV to EBITDA and market capitalization above roughly 2 billion dollars, and finally focusing on the 100 stocks with the highest EV to EBITDA (i.e., the most expensive on that metric). From this universe it seeks to capture intermediate horizon trend continuation by ranking securities on approximately 200 trading day rate of change using daily history and current price, then buying the top 10 names and holding them equal weight until the next rebalance.

Risk control is implemented via a market timing overlay: if SPY is below its 120 day simple moving average, the portfolio exits equities and allocates 100 percent to TLT; otherwise it remains fully invested in the selected equity basket. Decisions are made once per month (month start, shortly after the open) using daily price and fundamental data, with implied turnover concentrated at the scheduled rebalance. The intended edge is cross sectional momentum applied within a liquid, fundamentally screened universe, combined with a defensive trend filter to reduce exposure in weak equity regimes.

raw.name: Monthly Momentum Stocks TLT Risk-Off Filter
raw.description:
The strategy is a monthly value plus momentum equity selection model with an explicit regime filter that shifts risk into long duration Treasuries during equity drawdown conditions. Each month it forms a U.S. large cap universe by first taking the most liquid stocks above 5 dollars, then restricting to firms with positive EV to EBITDA and market capitalization above roughly 2 billion dollars, and finally focusing on the 100 stocks with the highest EV to EBITDA (i.e., the most expensive on that metric). From this universe it seeks to capture intermediate horizon trend continuation by ranking securities on approximately 200 trading day rate of change using daily history and current price, then buying the top 10 names and holding them equal weight until the next rebalance.

Risk control is implemented via a market timing overlay: if SPY is below its 120 day simple moving average, the portfolio exits equities and allocates 100 percent to TLT; otherwise it remains fully invested in the selected equity basket. Decisions are made once per month (month start, shortly after the open) using daily price and fundamental data, with implied turnover concentrated at the scheduled rebalance. The intended edge is cross sectional momentum applied within a liquid, fundamentally screened universe, combined with a defensive trend filter to reduce exposure in weak equity regimes.
